content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,808.40, up 21.40 points or 0.24%. Tech led the charge as software names surged, masking weakness in gold and energy. The index remains 4.29% below its 52-week high, with the five-day trend still down 1.76%. #ASX #ausbiz #equities

Post 2
Information Technology surged 5.21%, the clear session winner. Health Care added 2.14%. Energy fell 1.04% and Materials dropped 0.63%, dragged by falling gold stocks and softer commodity sentiment. Two sectors pulling in opposite directions defined the day. #ASX #ausbiz #technology #energy #materials

Post 3
WiseTech Global was the standout, closing at AUD 32.86, up 14.26% or AUD 4.10. That single move was enough to shift the entire IT sector. Xero added 8.17% to AUD 70.31, confirming the rotation into local software names was broad and conviction-driven. #ASX #ausbiz #technology #software

Post 4
Gold in AUD terms sits at AUD 5,927.36 per oz, silver at AUD 90.13 per oz, platinum at AUD 2,459.42 per oz, and palladium at AUD 1,933.90 per oz. The AUD/USD rate of 0.6898 is amplifying commodity price moves for local producers. #ASX #gold #silver #commodities #ausbiz

Post 5
Gold stocks were the session's biggest drag despite AUD gold holding above AUD 5,900 per oz. Watch whether that price level holds overnight — if USD gold softens, AUD producers face a double squeeze with AUD/USD at 0.6898 offering limited cushion. #ASX #gold #mining #ausbiz #commodities
